title: Greater Peoria Sanitary District Finalizes Agreements With Burnham RNG to Produce Renewable Natural Gas
content:
The Greater Peoria Sanitary District (GPSD) has finalized agreements with Burnham RNG to convert methane from its wastewater treatment facilities into renewable natural gas (RNG), reinforcing GPSDâ€™s commitment to sustainability and innovation. This initiative captures methaneâ€”a natural byproduct of wastewater treatmentâ€”and transforms it into clean fuel, reducing greenhouse gas emissions while generating value for customers and the broader community.

Brian Johnson, GPSDâ€™s Executive Director, praised Burnhamâ€™s responsiveness and highlighted the project as a â€œgame changerâ€ that turns waste into a renewable energy source. Chris Tynan, Burnham RNGâ€™s founder, emphasized the collaboration as a model for how public and private entities can partner to unlock biogas potential, advance energy independence, and deliver environmental and community benefits.

The project is expected to provide long-term economic and environmental advantages, positioning GPSD as a leader in sustainable utility innovation. Details will be shared at WEFTEC 2025 during an Interactive Technical Session on September 30 in Chicago, where media representatives are invited to learn more.
author: Analysts Account
